description: Shopping cart and checkout service ecosystem — manages cart state, order placement, payment orchestration, and fulfillment handoff for Omni-Mart.

services.name: Cart Service
services.description: Manages shopping cart state: add/remove items, apply coupons, calculate subtotals. Persists cart in Redis with SQLite fallback.

services.name: Checkout Orchestrator
services.description: Saga coordinator for order placement: reserves inventory, authorizes payment, confirms order, and triggers fulfillment. Failure at any step triggers compensating transactions.

services.name: Payment Gateway
services.description: PCI-DSS compliant gateway proxying card authorizations and captures to external payment processors. Tokenizes card data; no raw PAN stored.

services.name: Inventory Sync
services.description: Real-time inventory position service. Consumes warehouse events and maintains available-to-promise (ATP) counts per SKU per location.

services.name: Fulfillment Router
services.description: Determines optimal fulfillment path (ship-from-store, ship-from-DC, or pickup) based on inventory position, cost, and SLA.

artifacts.title: Cart Service – Ownership & On-Call
artifacts.content: Cart Service is owned by Commerce Team (T1). On-call: commerce-oncall@omnimart.dev. The service handles ~8k RPM at peak. Cart state lives in Redis cluster with 15-min TTL SQLite write-behind for persistence. Commerce Team also owns Checkout Orchestrator (T0 — revenue-critical).

artifacts.title: Checkout Orchestrator – Saga Runbook
artifacts.content: Checkout saga steps: (1) Reserve inventory via Inventory Sync, (2) Authorize payment via Payment Gateway, (3) Confirm order, (4) Trigger fulfillment via Fulfillment Router, (5) Send confirmation via Notification Hub. Compensating actions: cancel reservation, void authorization, mark order failed. Deploy requires Payments Team sign-off if touching payment step. Blast radius: any saga failure blocks revenue.

artifacts.title: Payment Gateway – PCI Compliance Note
artifacts.content: Payment Gateway is the only service in PCI scope. Card data is tokenized at ingestion; raw PAN never crosses service boundary. Deploying Payment Gateway requires security review from InfoSec. Downstream dependency: external payment processor (Stripe/Adyen). Latency SLA: authorization response < 2s p99. Blast radius on outage: all checkouts blocked.

artifacts.title: Inventory Sync – Dependency Map
artifacts.content: Inventory Sync consumes real-time warehouse events from ext-warehouse-feed (Kafka). Serves ATP queries to Cart Service (stock check on add-to-cart), Checkout Orchestrator (reservation), and Catalog API (in-stock badge). If Inventory Sync is unavailable: Cart shows stale stock, Checkout may over-sell, Catalog badges are incorrect. Recovery: replay from Kafka offset.

artifacts.title: Checkout – Post-Incident Review (PIR-2026-031)
artifacts.content: Incident: Checkout saga timed out for 12% of orders during flash sale. Root cause: Inventory Sync ATP queries exceeded 3s p99 due to warehouse event backpressure. Checkout Orchestrator saga timeout was 5s; double-booking occurred when timeout hit between reserve and authorize steps. Resolution: increased saga timeout to 8s, added circuit breaker on Inventory Sync, implemented idempotent reservation keys. Lesson: blast radius of Inventory Sync latency extends to revenue loss.
